blocks.body: Purpose: to / in order to / so that shows you how to talk about purpose through an infinitive structure or a full clause.

{"level":"B1-B2","intro":"When we talk about purpose, we can use a short infinitive structure or a full clause. The choice depends on whether the same person performs both actions.","rules":[{"label":"to / in order to + verb","color":"emerald","text":"These structures often work when the subject of both actions is the same.","example":"She went to the shop to buy milk."},{"label":"so that + clause","color":"blue","text":"So that is useful when we need a separate subject or a modal meaning.","example":"I wrote it down so that I wouldn't forget."},{"label":"Same aim, different grammar","color":"amber","text":"All three options can express purpose, but they are not interchangeable in every sentence.","example":"He spoke slowly in order to be clear."}]}

blocks.heading: 1. to / in order to
blocks.body:
To + verb is the simplest way to express purpose.

  • I opened the window to get some air.
  • She saved money in order to study abroad.

In order to sounds slightly more formal or more emphatic, but the pattern is the same.

blocks.heading: 2. so that + clause
blocks.body:
We use so that when the purpose needs a separate subject or a modal verb.

  • I left a note so that she could find the keys.
  • We whispered so that nobody would hear us.

This structure is especially useful when another person performs the second action or when the result matters.

blocks.heading: 3. Choosing the pattern
blocks.body:
MeaningPatternExample
same subjectto / in order to + verbShe called to ask a question.
different subject or modal meaningso that + clauseShe called so that I could help her.
more explicit formal emphasisin order to + verbThey left early in order to avoid traffic.

blocks.heading: 4. Common mistakes
blocks.body:
• Mistake: putting a full clause with a subject after to. After to, we need only the verb.
  • Mistake: forgetting that so that needs its own subject and verb.
  • Mistake: treating in order to as the only correct option. In many cases, simple to sounds more natural.

blocks.heading: 5. Quick rule
blocks.body:
• For simple purpose with the same subject, use to + verb.
  • In order to adds a slightly more formal or more explicit tone.
  • For a separate subject or modal meaning, use so that + clause.
  • First choose the structure you need, then choose the linker.
